title: link_ctx key reload overwrites old secrets without wiping
description:
Trigger: lichen_link_load_key() derives a new Ed25519 scalar directly into ctx->ed25519_sk at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:95-106, and lichen_link_load_link_key() copies a new AES key directly into ctx->link_key at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:230-238. Neither path checks ctx->has_key/ctx->has_link_key nor securely wipes the existing key bytes before overwriting them.

Impact: key rotation or reprovisioning can leave previous signing/link keys recoverable in RAM until those bytes are naturally reused, defeating the module's cleanup guarantees and increasing post-compromise key exposure. This is distinct from lichen_link_cleanup(), which wipes only when callers explicitly tear down the context at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:289-295.

Relevant refs: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:102, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:119, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:237, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/link_ctx.c:293.
